title: Use containers/storage/pkg/regexp in place of regexp
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Just to throw out the idea:

It may be worth exploring whether some of the regexes are actually needed.  When writing the first version of [vgrep](https://github.com/vrothberg/vgrep) (in Python) I noticed that regexes were a _major_ performance bottle neck.  Regexes can be powerful for parsing but if they're used for filtering, a `string.Contains` is usually much faster - extremes excluded.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> The changes LGTM but there are still some candidates left in the code:
> 
> ```
> podman (regexp) $ vgrep --no-ripgrep --no-git -w regexp --exclude-dir=vendor --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=test
> Index File                                Line Content
>     0 cmd/podman/images/trust_set.go         6 "regexp"
>     1 cmd/podman/images/trust_set.go        76 reg := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+\/?:?[0-9]*[a-z0-9-\/:]*$`)
>     2 cmd/podman/images/trust_set.go        81 reg = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9-:\./]*$`)
>     3 cmd/podman/inspect/inspect.go          9 "regexp"
>     4 cmd/podman/inspect/inspect.go        265 m := regexp.MustCompile(`{{\s*\.Id\s*}}`)
>     5 cmd/podman/parse/net.go               13 "github.com/containers/storage/pkg/regexp"
>     6 cmd/podman/parse/net.go               26 alphaRegexp  = regexp.Delayed(`[a-zA-Z]`)
>     7 cmd/podman/parse/net.go               27 domainRegexp = regexp.Delayed(`^(:?(:?[a-zA-Z0-9]|(:?[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9\-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]))(:?\.(:?[a-zA-Z0-9]|(:?[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9\-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])))*)\.?\s*$`)
>     8 cmd/podman/system/connection/add.go    8 "regexp"
>     9 cmd/podman/system/connection/add.go  109 if match, err := regexp.Match("^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9+.-]*://", []byte(dest)); err != nil {
>    10 cmd/podman/system/connection/add.go  206 if match, err := regexp.Match("^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9+.-]*://", []byte(dest)); err != nil {
>    11 cmd/podman/machine/machine.go         11 "regexp"
>    12 cmd/podman/machine/machine.go        104 re := regexp.MustCompile(`machine_events.*\.sock`)
>    13 cmd/podman-mac-helper/main.go         12 "regexp"
>    14 cmd/podman-mac-helper/main.go        117 re := regexp.MustCompile(`\r?\n`)
>    15 libpod/oci_util.go                     7 "regexp"
>    16 libpod/oci_util.go                   141 if match := regexp.MustCompile("(?i).*permission denied.*|.*operation not permitted.*").FindString(runtimeMsg); match != "" {
>    17 libpod/oci_util.go                   148 if match := regexp.MustCompile("(?i).*executable file not found in.*|.*no such file or directory.*").FindString(runtimeMsg); match != "" {
>    18 libpod/oci_util.go                   155 if match := regexp.MustCompile("`/proc/[a-z0-9-].+/attr.*`").FindString(runtimeMsg); match != "" {
>    19 libpod/networking_common.go            9 "regexp"
>    20 libpod/networking_common.go          175 b, rerr := regexp.MatchString("Couldn't load target `CNI-[a-f0-9]{24}':No such file or directory|Chain 'CNI-[a-f0-9]{24}' does not exist", err.Error())
>    21 libpod/options.go                     26 "github.com/containers/storage/pkg/regexp"
>    22 libpod/options.go                     32 var umaskRegex = regexp.Delayed(`^[0-7]{1,4}$`)
>    23 pkg/util/utils.go                     12 "regexp"
>    24 pkg/util/utils.go                     92 m, err := regexp.MatchString(r, s)
>    25 pkg/bindings/images/build.go          27 "github.com/containers/storage/pkg/regexp"
>    26 pkg/bindings/images/build.go          39 var iidRegex = regexp.Delayed(`^[0-9a-f]{12}`)
>    27 pkg/domain/filters/volumes.go          6 "regexp"
>    28 pkg/domain/filters/volumes.go         20 nameRegexp, err := regexp.Compile(val)
>    29 pkg/ps/ps.go                           8 "regexp"
>    30 pkg/ps/ps.go                         322 reg := regexp.MustCompile(`.*\[|\].*`)
>    31 pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go     12 "regexp"
>    32 pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go    870 fieldPathLabelRegex := regexp.MustCompile(fieldPathLabelPattern)
>    33 pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go    872 fieldPathAnnotationRegex := regexp.MustCompile(fieldPathAnnotationPattern)
>    34 pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go         9 "github.com/containers/storage/pkg/regexp"
>    35 pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go        77 validPortRange = regexp.Delayed(`\d+(-\d+)?(/udp|/tcp)?$`)

but these are already created when needed and not at "init" time, so it should not be a problem?

> but these are already created when needed and not at "init" time, so it should not be a problem?

D'oh ... you're absolutely right.  I'll grab a fresh coffee.

/lgtm
